_comment: Authoritative cog rig from the 2D sprite rigger 'copy export'. VALIDATED: my compositor (tools/rig_preview.py) reproduces the rigger's own headless composite pixel-faithfully. Reference frame = rigger ORIGINAL layer, 866x848. Per part: pivotRef = where its bone sits in the ref frame at rest; scale = part-art-px -> ref-px; restRot deg (CSS/CW positive); boneArt = rotation center in the part's OWN art px. The legset is CUT into 3 legs, each hinging about ITS OWN hip (user placed only the rear hip; front hips = that relationship rotated about the hub, NOT the same coord reused). All 3 wheels are the SAME tire, castering about their axle (47,132); only the mount differs.

nativeScale._note: art-native-px -> common-frame factor; multiply by the size dial. NOT the raw export scale.

forkCenters._note: wheel mount = the U-fork opening center per leg, in legset 1031-art px (foot point).

TUNED._note: Maxwell-approved feel (2026-07-22). These are the numbers to port to sim.nim.
TUNED.model: splay = one-sided; rest = both front legs tucked restTuck inward; steering-side leg swings OUT up to splayAmount, scaled by |turn velocity| = |heading angular velocity|/WFULL (decoupled from bodyTurnRate); wheels caster to per-foot velocity; arms gated on carryingFlag; plasma gated on hasPlasmaArc
